items.title: Microgreens sold in Ontario, Quebec recalled due to possible E. coli contamination
items.summary_auto: Several types of organic microgreens sold in Ontario and Quebec under the Farm Boy and Kyan Culture brands have been recalled due to potential E. coli contamination, the Canadian Food Inspection Agency says.

items.title: Canadian in isolation tests presumptively positive for hantavirus, B.C.'s top doctor says
items.summary_auto: A Canadian isolating in B.C. has presumptively tested positive for hantavirus after leaving the cruise ship affected by an outbreak of the Andes strain in recent weeks, B.C. 's top doctor said Saturday.

items.title: ISIS second-in-command killed by U.S. and Nigerian forces, presidents say
items.summary_auto: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command ‌of ISIS globally, has been killed in an operation conducted by U.S. and Nigerian forces in the northeast of the African country, U.S. President Donald Trump and his counterpart in Nigeria, Bola Ahmed Tinubu, said.

items.title: Thousands join rival ‘Unite the Kingdom’ and pro-Palestine protests in London
items.summary_auto: Tens of thousands of protesters attended rival marches in London — the 'Unite the Kingdom' rally, organized by far-right figure Tommy Robinson, and a pro-Palestinian demonstration marking 78 years since the Nakba. More than 4,000 police officers were deployed to prevent confrontations.

items.title: Paul Boutilier, Stanley Cup champ and former curling executive, remembered for his humility
items.summary_auto: A Nova Scotian who won a Stanley Cup and a World Junior Hockey Championship — and later worked as a university professor and curling executive — is being remembered for his humility. Paul (Boots) Boutilier was 63.

items.title: Humpback whale released after spectacular rescue effort found dead off Denmark
items.summary_auto: A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as the animal released two weeks ago in a spectacular and controversial rescue effort after repeatedly becoming stranded off Germany's Baltic Sea coast, Danish authorities said Saturday.

items.title: Canadiens could advance to Eastern Conference final with a Game 6 win over Sabres tonight
items.summary_auto: Series winner will face the Carolina Hurricanes in the Eastern Conference final.

items.title: At least 80 deaths reported as Congo grapples with new Ebola outbreak
items.summary_auto: At least 80 deaths have been reported in Congo's new Ebola disease outbreak in the eastern Ituri province, authorities said, as health workers raced Saturday to intensify screening and contact tracing to contain the disease.

items.title: Israel kills Hamas military wing leader in strike on Gaza
items.summary_auto: An Israeli airstrike on Gaza killed the chief of Hamas's military wing, the most senior official from the Palestinian militant group killed by Israel since an October U.S.-backed ceasefire agreement that was meant to halt fighting.

items.title: Multimillionaire Vancouver developer jailed for ignoring court orders
items.summary_auto: Developer Helen Chan Sun is the sole shareholder of a company that holds the title to hundreds of millions of dollars worth of Vancouver real estate. But the multimillionaire will spend the next 40 days in a jail cell after repeatedly ignoring orders to come clean with a creditor about her finances.

items.title: Gen Z has dubbed Jessica the new Karen. Here are my thoughts as a Karen
items.summary_auto: The name Karen has become synonymous with rude and racist behaviour, unreasonable expectations and undue entitlement. But for Karen Scanlan, it’s just her name.

items.title: In spies he trusts? In Cuba, Trump looks again to CIA chief, rather than a diplomat, to deliver a message
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump's maximum pressure campaign on Cuba now includes overtly dispatching a spy chief to Havana to ensure that his demands are clear.

items.title: Ontario should lower colon cancer screening age even further, say patients and advocates
items.summary_auto: Ontario has lowered the screening age for colon cancer to 45 years old from 50. But patients and advocates say with the rising number of cases in young people, the age should be even lower.

items.title: Manitoba's social media ban could bar teachers using YouTube in the classroom, says Kinew
items.summary_auto: Manitoba Premier Wab Kinew says his province's ban on social media and AI chatbots for kids will likely bar teachers using YouTube in the classroom — a move that comes as the federal government continues to deliberate over whether to enact its own restrictions.

items.title: Pair of First Nations in northwestern Ontario declare joint state of emergency over drug trafficking
items.summary_auto: Two neighbouring First Nations in northwestern Ontario have declared a joint state of emergency “due to the rising crime and violence trespassing into their communities.” Long Lake #58 First Nation and Ginoogaming First Nation, located about 300 kilometres northeast of Thunder Bay off Highway 11, have reported escalating concerns about drug trafficking and gang-related violence. Here's how they're taking matters into their own hands.

items.title: Hantavirus has no cure. Here's where researchers are at with treatments and vaccines
items.summary_auto: Researchers across the world are working on a hantavirus vaccine, though they say development is in the early stages and rollout could be years away.

items.title: White House points to 'longstanding unfair trade practices' when asked about Gordie Howe bridge opening
items.summary_auto: For months, Canadian officials have said that the long-awaited opening of the $6.4 billion Gordie Howe International Bridge is dependent on various testing and commissioning tasks. But fresh comments from a Canadian cabinet member as well as two top U.S. officials suggest the current trade war is playing a role in the new border crossing's opening timeline.

items.title: 300 people, 75 projectors, 1 night: Inside Drake's audacious CN Tower light show
items.summary_auto: Though he's teamed with Drake a few times now, Anil Mohabir is still in a dreamlike state after their latest collaboration: a dramatic, 'real-time, 3D animation' projection that sent an icy blue wave of light up the CN Tower overnight ahead of the Toronto rapper's surprise triple-album drop.
